refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify it remains within the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS prioritization.
DP3: Integrate real-time packet loss monitoring and dynamic adjustment algorithms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and network link quality.
Dependency on real-time monitoring data for adaptation.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk that adaptive protocol may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating latency monitoring as part of the adaptive protocol to mitigate responsiveness risks.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, communication management, and real-time data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send communication quality status and packet loss metrics to NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands from NGHE Solution or operator.

module_containment_scores.notes: Contains overall system but less focused on communication protocol specifics.
Primarily physical control, minimal communication protocol responsibility.
Highest containment score due to direct responsibility for teleoperation communication.
Focuses on safety alerts, less on communication quality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with NGHE Solution for telemetry reporting.
Risk of insufficient interface definition leading to integration issues.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface contracts between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution for telemetry and tasking to reduce integration risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with a clear mechanism to control packet loss.

diffusion_risks: Minor risk of telemetry data duplication between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment for the DP with minimal diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Teleoperation interfaces under test conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Enhance interface definitions between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution for telemetry and tasking.
Include latency monitoring in future DP refinements to address responsiveness.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a viable DP, and assigns it to an appropriate IM with clear interfaces and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; recommended revisions are for future refinement.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Adaptive communication protocol can meet latency requirements.
open_risks: Latency impact of adaptive protocol on teleoperation responsiveness.
Interface clarity between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution.
evidence_needed: Test results demonstrating packet loss within limits under network conditions.
Latency measurements during teleoperation with adaptive protocol.
